input.headline: Why nonalcoholic spirits go from strength to strength

raw.headline: Why nonalcoholic spirits go from strength to strength

winner: Even though they can't get you drunk, non-alcoholic spirits are so strong...they can make your sober friends think they're actually having a good time!

candidates.text: Why did the sober gin bottle feel so proud? Because it was going from strength to strength... with a twist of lemon!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'strength to strength' and 'twist of lemon' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline while being concise and humorous."}

candidates.text: Even though they can't get you drunk, non-alcoholic spirits are so strong...they can make your sober friends think they're actually having a good time!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on 'strength,' specific to nonalcoholic spirits, concise, and naturally funny with a clever play on expectations."}

candidates.text: A spirit-free party just got stronger with more guests... apparently they're all teetotalers!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with wordplay on 'strength' and 'teetotalers,' but it lacks specificity and the connection to nonalcoholic spirits isn't clearly made."}

candidates.text: A barman, pouring a drink for a patron, says with complete seriousness, "I hear these nonalcoholic spirits are really going places.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'going places,' but it's a bit generic and lacks the sharp surprise or specificity that would make it stand out in a humor competition."}

candidates.text: A spiritless party is just a bunch of people standing around, asking "What's the point?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'spiritless,' but it's a bit generic and doesn't fully tie into the headline about nonalcoholic spirits. It lacks specificity and could be more surprising."}
